title: World's Longest Wooden Coaster
notes: At 7,359 ft, The Beast has held this record since opening day. Listed in the Guinness Book of World Records.

title: World's Tallest Wooden Coaster
notes: Held until Mean Streak at Cedar Point (161 ft) opened in 1994.

title: World's Fastest Wooden Coaster
notes: Held until Mean Streak at Cedar Point opened in 1994.

title: First Arrow Dynamics Launched Loop to Open to the Public
notes: Screamin' Demon opened April 16, 1977, as the first Arrow Launched Loop to operate publicly, and the first looping coaster at Kings Island. (Schwarzkopf/Intamin's shuttle loop debuted separately at Kings Dominion the same year.)

title: World's First Modern Suspended Roller Coaster
notes: The Bat opened April 26, 1981, as the first modern suspended coaster — cars hang below the track and swing freely. Arrow billed it as 'the first of its kind.' Earlier obscure attempts (e.g. Alpen-Flug, 1975) predated it but were not comparable in scale or design.

title: World's First Modern Purpose-Built Stand-Up Coaster
notes: King Cobra opened in 1984 as the world's first modern stand-up roller coaster with a vertical loop. Built by TOGO.

title: World's Tallest Full-Circuit Roller Coaster
notes: Vortex stood 148 ft at opening and claimed the record for tallest full-circuit coaster. Lost to Shockwave at Six Flags Great America (170 ft) in 1988.

title: World's Highest Drop on a Steel Coaster
notes: Vortex's 138 ft drop took this record from Mindbender at Galaxyland. Lost to Shockwave at Six Flags Great America in 1988.

title: First Roller Coaster with Six Inversions
notes: Vortex was the first coaster in the world to send riders through six inversions on a continuous circuit. Surpassed by Shockwave at Six Flags Great America (seven inversions) in 1988.

title: World's Tallest Wooden Coaster
notes: Son of Beast stood 218 ft at opening. Lost to Intimidator 305's wooden equivalent; specifically surpassed by Goliath-era coasters by the early 2010s.

title: World's Fastest Wooden Coaster
notes: Son of Beast reached 78.4 mph at opening. Record subsequently broken by faster wooden coasters in the 2010s.

title: World's Only Looping Wooden Coaster
notes: Son of Beast opened with a 118 ft steel vertical loop — the only wooden coaster in the world with a loop. The loop was removed prior to the 2007 season.

title: World's Longest Inverted Coaster
notes: Banshee's 4,124 ft of track makes it the longest inverted roller coaster in the world. Still held as of 2025.

title: 7th Giga Coaster Built Worldwide
notes: Orion (300 ft drop, 91 mph) was the 7th giga coaster constructed globally when it opened in 2020.

title: Kings Island's Tallest, Fastest, and Longest Steel Coaster
notes: At 287 ft tall, 91 mph, and 5,321 ft of track, Orion holds all three superlative records within the park for steel coasters.
